tweaks_applied.change: Added border: 1px solid var(--border-subtle) to .message-content code:not(pre code) — makes inline code chips visually distinct from surrounding prose, matching Claude.ai's style
Increased .message-content p line-height from 1.6 to 1.65 — subtly more breathing room between lines, measurably improves scan speed on dense responses
Bumped .code-header background from alpha-white-5 to alpha-white-8 and border from border-subtle to border-default — the language label and copy button now visually separate from the code body
Changed .code-block-wrapper border from border-subtle (5% alpha) to border-default (10% alpha) — code blocks now read as distinct cards rather than blending into the background

Upgraded left border from border-subtle to border-default, text color from text-tertiary to text-secondary, opacity from 0.7 to 0.8, font-size from 12px to 12.5px, line-height from 1.5 to 1.6 — thinking content is now readable without feeling noisy

Two changes: (a) background changed from gray-3 to gray-2 so the input lifts visually above the chat area; (b) added .message-composer-textarea:focus rule with box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--accent-subtle) + drop shadow — matches Claude.ai/ChatGPT premium focus ring feel

Changed TimestampLine color from text-[var(--gray-6)] (#3f3f46) to text-[var(--gray-7)] (#52525b) — still muted but readable on hover, prevents the metadata from being completely invisible at a glance

Replaced shadow-[var(--shadow-sm)] with .chat-user-bubble class that applies both the drop shadow AND inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.18) — a subtle top-edge highlight (Arc/Linear/Vercel-style) that makes the bubble feel three-dimensional and premium

Changed completed tool cards from border-[var(--border-subtle)] to border-[var(--border-default)] — cards now have a visible boundary at 10% alpha (vs 5%), making them feel like actual distinct UI elements rather than invisible containers

summary: 9 CSS-only or near-CSS-only tweaks focused on the chat area. The biggest perceived quality jumps: (1) the composer textarea now has a premium focus ring matching Claude.ai/ChatGPT; (2) inline code chips have a visible border making them pop from prose text; (3) code blocks have more visible headers and borders, reading as proper distinct cards; (4) user message bubbles have an inner-top highlight giving them a three-dimensional, premium feel; (5) tool cards at rest now have enough border contrast to scan as distinct UI elements. All changes are CSS variables-based and theme-compatible.
